skill.name: laravel-specialist
skill.description: Guides Laravel 10+ application work across Eloquent models, API resources, queues, Livewire components, Sanctum authentication, Horizon, and tests.
skill.summary: Build maintainable Laravel 10+ features with Eloquent, APIs, queues, Livewire, and testing guidance.
skill.author: jeffallan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced a high score because it matched Markdown code fences, Laravel method names, Artisan command examples, credential environment variable names, and documentation URLs. Review found no executable skill code, no prompt injection attempt, and no evidence of credential exfiltration or malicious network behavior. The residual risk is low because users may copy shell and configuration examples into real Laravel projects.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Flags Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer matched fenced Markdown examples and Laravel Artisan commands. These lines document commands for users to run in their own Laravel projects and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is inside Markdown tables or fenced code blocks. No repository file provides an executable script or automation path that invokes these commands.

low_findings.title: Credential Environment Variables Are Configuration References
low_findings.description: The AWS credential names appear in a Laravel SQS configuration example. The snippet reads standard environment variables and does not send secrets to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines define a queue driver configuration array. I found no evidence of credential logging, collection, or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Network and URL Flags Are Benign References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are an author metadata URL, a localhost CORS example, and a mocked test assertion. They do not indicate unauthorized outbound network activity by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are either metadata or documentation examples. The testing example uses a fake HTTP service and asserts expected behavior rather than initiating hidden traffic.

low_findings.title: Language Token Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The with-statement and weak-crypto alerts are token collisions with Laravel APIs and ordinary words. Examples include Eloquent with calls and description text, not JavaScript scope changes or cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are semantically Laravel query examples or YAML metadata. I found no actual JavaScript with statement and no weak cryptographic algorithm implementation.

low_findings.title: Broad Configuration Examples Require Project Review
low_findings.description: The CORS example allows all methods and headers while supporting credentials. This is a common teaching example, but production users should narrow it for their application.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The finding is about copied configuration risk, not malicious skill behavior. The snippet is clearly documentation, but it could be unsafe if used unchanged in production.

content.user_title: Build Laravel Features with Expert Guidance
content.value_statement: Laravel teams need consistent patterns for models, APIs, queues, Livewire components, and tests. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Laravel guidance for implementation work.
actual_capabilities: Guides Eloquent model design with relationships, scopes, casts, observers, and query optimization.
Helps create Laravel API routes, controllers, form requests, resources, middleware, and response patterns.
Provides queue job, worker, Horizon, batching, retry, and failure handling examples.
Supports Livewire component patterns for forms, validation, events, uploads, polling, and authorization.
Guides Laravel testing with feature tests, unit tests, factories, fakes, mocking, and API assertions.
Encourages Laravel security basics such as validation, authorization, eager loading, and protected configuration.
limitations: It is a reference skill and does not execute Laravel commands or inspect a running application by itself.
Examples must be adapted to the project version, package choices, database schema, and deployment environment.
It covers Laravel 10+ patterns and may need updates for future framework changes.
It does not replace a full security review for authentication, authorization, CORS, secrets, or production queues.

use_cases.title: Design a Laravel API feature
use_cases.description: Plan routes, form requests, controllers, resources, services, models, and tests for a new API feature.

use_cases.title: Improve Eloquent performance
use_cases.description: Review model relationships, eager loading, scopes, counts, chunks, lazy collections, and indexes for cleaner data access.

use_cases.title: Add reliable background processing
use_cases.description: Create jobs, batching, retry behavior, failed job handling, worker settings, and Horizon monitoring patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Model Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the laravel-specialist skill to create a Laravel 10 feature for [feature]. Include the Eloquent model, migration, controller, request validation, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Build a REST API Resource
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the laravel-specialist skill to design a versioned API for [resource]. Include routes, controller actions, form requests, API resources, authorization checks, and feature tests.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Eloquent Queries
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the laravel-specialist skill to review these Laravel models and queries for N+1 issues, missing relationships, scopes, casts, indexes, and safer query structure.

prompt_templates.title: Design Queue Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the laravel-specialist skill to design a production queue workflow for [business process]. Include jobs, batching, retries, idempotency, failure handling, Horizon settings, and tests.

output_examples.input: Create a Laravel API for publishing posts with validation and authorization.
output: A route plan with public and protected endpoints.
A controller and form request structure with clear validation rules.
API resource guidance for conditional fields and relationships.
Feature test coverage for guests, owners, invalid data, and successful publishing.

output_examples.input: Optimize a slow posts index that loads authors and comments.
output: A diagnosis of likely N+1 queries and over-fetching.
Recommended eager loading, counts, pagination, and selected columns.
Model scope suggestions for published and popular posts.
Testing notes for query behavior and response structure.

output_examples.input: Move image processing into a Laravel queue.
output: A job design with retries, timeout, backoff, and idempotency guidance.
Dispatch timing recommendations for database commits and user responses.
Horizon worker settings and failed job monitoring notes.
Queue fake test coverage for dispatch and failure behavior.

best_practices: Provide the Laravel version, database engine, authentication package, and relevant models before asking for implementation help.
Ask for tests with every generated feature, including validation, authorization, success paths, and failure paths.
Review generated configuration examples before production use, especially queues, CORS, secrets, and worker limits.
anti_patterns: Do not paste generic examples into production without adapting authorization, validation, and environment settings.
Do not ask for controllers that contain all business logic when services, jobs, policies, or resources are needed.
Do not skip database performance checks for relationships, eager loading, pagination, and indexes.

faq.question: Which Laravel versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Laravel 10+ and modern PHP 8.2+ patterns.

faq.question: Can it generate complete Laravel feature plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline models, migrations, controllers, requests, resources, services, jobs, and tests.

faq.question: Does it run Artisan commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents command examples, but it does not execute commands by itself.

faq.question: Does it cover Livewire?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for components, forms, validation, uploads, events, polling, authorization, and performance.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers PHPUnit, Pest, factories, feature tests, unit tests, fakes, mocks, and API assertions.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples, not malicious behavior, with low residual copy-and-adapt risk.
